People who hate those who worship God often pretend to be religious so they can more easily harm them. Just as death often comes when people least expect it, we should constantly examine whether we are in a state of mind that we would want to be found in when called to appear before our Judge. Sometimes the price for someone’s life is their wealth. But those who try to bribe death, saying, “Do not kill us, because we have treasures,” will find themselves greatly deceived. This sad history warns us never to feel secure in this world. We can never be sure of peace on this side of heaven.

Evil deeds cannot last long, and no one can prosper who hardens their heart against God. And those lose comfort in real fears if they excuse their sins by pretending to be afraid. When a wise and peaceful leader is removed and replaced by someone rash and ambitious, it affects the well-being of many. Only those are happy and stable who fear the Lord and walk in his ways.
